what: Chart Generation for Microsoft Word, Microsoft Excel and Microsoft PowerPoint Documents
title: Chart Generation for Microsoft Word, Microsoft Excel and Microsoft PowerPoint Documents
text: mschart package provides framework easily creating charts ‘Microsoft PowerPoint’ presentations, ‘Microsoft Word’ documents ‘Microsoft Excel’ workbooks. used package officer produce charts new existing PowerPoint, Word Excel files. ‘Microsoft Charts’, data integrated document linked chart. result can edited, annotated resized. data updated document, chart also updated.

title: Chart Generation for Microsoft Word, Microsoft Excel and Microsoft PowerPoint Documents
text: Classical charts (Office 2007+): ms_barchart(), ms_linechart(), ms_areachart(), ms_scatterchart(), ms_piechart(), ms_bubblechart(), ms_radarchart(), ms_stockchart(). chartEx charts (Office 2016+): ms_boxplotchart(), ms_funnelchart(), ms_histogramchart(), ms_paretochart(), ms_sunburstchart(), ms_treemapchart(), ms_waterfallchart(). Classical charts can combined plot area ms_chart_combine().

title: Chart Generation for Microsoft Word, Microsoft Excel and Microsoft PowerPoint Documents
text: basic example shows create bar chart. use package officer send object chart. chart object can dropped Excel sheet officer::sheet_add_drawing(). default chart’s underlying data written next chart automatically. can also write data first officer::sheet_write_data() ask chart reuse passing write_data = FALSE. useful data appear specific position, several charts share dataset: Use print(your_chart, preview = TRUE) open chart PowerPoint iterating.
code: library(mschart)  sales <- data.frame(   quarter = rep(c("Q1", "Q2", "Q3", "Q4"), each = 2),   revenue = c(12, 9, 15, 11, 18, 14, 21, 17),   region  = rep(c("EU", "US"), times = 4) )  bars <- ms_barchart(   data = sales, x = "quarter", y = "revenue", group = "region" ) library(officer) doc <- read_pptx() doc <- add_slide(doc, layout = "Title and Content", master = "Office Theme") doc <- ph_with(doc, value = bars, location = ph_location_fullsize())  print(doc, target = "example.pptx") wb <- read_xlsx() wb <- add_sheet(wb, label = "sales")  # 1. Write the data on the sheet at the desired position. wb <- sheet_write_data(wb, sheet = "sales", value = bars$data_series,                        start_col = 1, start_row = 1)  # 2. Add the chart and tell it to reference the data already in place. wb <- sheet_add_drawing(wb, sheet = "sales", value = bars,                         write_data = FALSE,                         start_col = 1, start_row = 1,                         left = 5, top = 0.5, width = 6, height = 4)  print(wb, target = "example.xlsx")

title: X axis settings — chart_ax_x
text: Define settings x axis. S3 generic; default method documented . ChartEx charts leaner set supported options - see package documentation.

title: X axis settings — chart_ax_x
code: chart_ax_x(x, ...)  # Default S3 method chart_ax_x(   x,   orientation,   crosses,   cross_between,   major_tick_mark,   minor_tick_mark,   tick_label_pos,   display,   num_fmt,   rotation,   limit_min,   limit_max,   position,   major_unit,   minor_unit,   major_time_unit,   minor_time_unit,   ... )

title: X axis settings — chart_ax_x
text: x ms_chart object. ... arguments passed S3 methods. orientation axis orientation, one 'maxMin', 'minMax'. crosses specifies axis crosses perpendicular axis, one 'autoZero', 'max', 'min'. cross_between specifies value axis crosses category axis categories, one '', 'midCat'. major_tick_mark, minor_tick_mark tick marks position, one 'cross', '', 'none', ''. tick_label_pos ticks labels position, one 'high', 'low', 'nextTo', 'none'. display axis displayed (logical length 1). num_fmt number formatting. See num_fmt section details. rotation rotation angle. Value -360 360. limit_min minimum value axis. Date objects also accepted converted automatically. limit_max maximum value axis. Date objects also accepted converted automatically. position value axis crosses perpendicular axis. major_unit numeric, interval major ticks gridlines. minor_unit numeric, interval minor ticks gridlines. major_time_unit time unit major ticks date axes, one "days", "months", "years". minor_time_unit time unit minor ticks date axes, one "days", "months", "years".

text: ms_chart object.

title: X axis settings — chart_ax_x
text: % need doubled, 0%% means "number percent symbol". current knowledge, depending chart type options, following values systematically used office chart engine; .e. chart pre-compute percentages, seems using 0%% effect. General: default value 0: display number decimal 0.00: display number two decimals 0%%: display percentages 0.00%%: display percentages two decimal places #,##0 #,##0.00 0.00E+00 # ?/? # ??/?? mm-dd-yy d-mmm-yy d-mmm mmm-yy h:mm /PM h:mm:ss /PM h:mm h:mm:ss m/d/yy h:mm #,##0 ;(#,##0) #,##0 ;[Red](#,##0) #,##0.00;(#,##0.00) #,##0.00;[Red](#,##0.00) mm:ss [h]:mm:ss mmss.0 ##0.0E+0 @

code: library(mschart)  chart_01 <- ms_linechart(   data = us_indus_prod,   x = "date", y = "value",   group = "type" )  chart_01 <- chart_ax_y(x = chart_01, limit_min = 20, limit_max = 120) chart_01 #> * 'ms_linechart' object #>  #> * original data [256,3] (sample): #>         date       type value #> 1 1960-01-01 unadjusted  40.2 #> 2 1960-04-01 unadjusted  39.8 #> 3 1960-07-01 unadjusted  38.5 #> 4 1960-10-01 unadjusted  38.0 #> 5 1961-01-01 unadjusted  37.1 #>  #> * series data [128,3] (sample): #>         date unadjusted adjusted #> 1 1960-01-01       40.2     40.5 #> 2 1960-04-01       39.8     40.0 #> 3 1960-07-01       38.5     39.0 #> 4 1960-10-01       38.0     38.1 #> 5 1961-01-01       37.1     37.6  # control axis intervals chart_01 <- chart_ax_x(chart_01,   major_unit = 10, major_time_unit = "years" ) chart_01 <- chart_ax_y(chart_01, major_unit = 20) chart_01 #> * 'ms_linechart' object #>  #> * original data [256,3] (sample): #>         date       type value #> 1 1960-01-01 unadjusted  40.2 #> 2 1960-04-01 unadjusted  39.8 #> 3 1960-07-01 unadjusted  38.5 #> 4 1960-10-01 unadjusted  38.0 #> 5 1961-01-01 unadjusted  37.1 #>  #> * series data [128,3] (sample): #>         date unadjusted adjusted #> 1 1960-01-01       40.2     40.5 #> 2 1960-04-01       39.8     40.0 #> 3 1960-07-01       38.5     39.0 #> 4 1960-10-01       38.0     38.1 #> 5 1961-01-01       37.1     37.6

title: Modify fill colour — chart_data_fill
text: x ms_chart object. values character(num series|1): set colour values map data values . named vector, values matched based names. contains one colour, colour associated existing series. update_stroke TRUE (default), series stroke colour also updated values, filled shape border match. Series whose stroke set "transparent" (example ms_areachart() ms_piechart() constructors) left untouched preserve deliberate "border" default. Set FALSE keep current stroke colours untouched manage independently via chart_data_stroke().

title: Modify data labels settings — chart_data_labels
code: chart_data_labels(x, ...)  # Default S3 method chart_data_labels(   x,   num_fmt = "General",   position = "ctr",   show_legend_key = FALSE,   show_val = FALSE,   show_cat_name = FALSE,   show_serie_name = FALSE,   show_percent = FALSE,   separator = ", ",   ... )

title: Modify data labels settings — chart_data_labels
text: x ms_chart object. ... arguments passed S3 methods. num_fmt character(1): number formatting specifies number format properties indicate format render numeric values. can "General", "0.00", "#,##0", "#,##0.00", "mm-dd-yy", "m/d/yy h:mm", etc. position character(1): specifies position data label. one 'b', 'ctr', 'inBase', 'inEnd', 'l', 'outEnd', 'r', 't'. grouping 'clustered', one 'ctr','inBase','inEnd','outEnd'. grouping 'stacked', one 'ctr','inBase','inEnd'. grouping 'standard', one 'b','ctr','l','r','t'. show_legend_key show legend key TRUE. show_val show values TRUE. show_cat_name show categories TRUE. show_serie_name show names series TRUE. show_percent show percentages TRUE. separator separator label components (value, category name, series name, etc.) multiple components displayed. Default ", ".

title: Set chart options — chart_settings
text: x ms_chart object. ... unused parameter vary_colors TRUE, data point single series displayed different color. gap_width gap appears bar clustered bars category bar chart. default width gap 150 percent bar width. can set 0 500 percent bar width. dir direction bars chart, value must one "horizontal" "vertical". grouping grouping series. barchart one "percentStacked", "clustered", "standard" "stacked". linechart areachart one "percentStacked", "standard" "stacked" ("clustered" bar-). overlap bar chart two series, bars category clustered together. default, bars directly adjacent . bars can made overlap space using overlap property. values range -100 100, representing percentage bar width overlap adjacent bars. setting -100 creates gap full bar width setting 100 causes bars category superimposed. default value 0. table TRUE set table barchart. style Style linechart scatterchart type markers. One 'none', 'line', 'lineMarker', 'marker', 'smooth', 'smoothMarker'. hi_low_lines officer::fp_border() high-low lines. Set FALSE hide . up_bars_fill fill colour bars (OHLC , close > open). up_bars_border officer::fp_border() bar borders. down_bars_fill fill colour bars (OHLC , close < open). down_bars_border officer::fp_border() bar borders. bubble3D logical, use 3D effect bubbles. hole_size size hole doughnut chart, 0 90 (percent radius). Default 0 produces pie chart; values 0 produce doughnut chart. line stroke cumulative percentage line. One : NULL (default, matches Excel-native: theme accent2 colour scaled chart palette), FALSE suppress line override (line depends chartstyle sidecar may render invisible), officer::fp_border().

title: Boxplot chart object — ms_boxplotchart
text: Creation box--whisker chart object can inserted 'Microsoft' document. Boxplot charts use chartEx pipeline (Office 2016+); older versions 'Microsoft Office' display fallback placeholder. Data long format: one row per observation. Office computes quartiles whiskers raw values; pre-aggregate.

title: Boxplot chart object — ms_boxplotchart
code: ms_boxplotchart(   data,   x,   y,   quartile_method = c("exclusive", "inclusive"),   show_mean_marker = TRUE,   show_mean_line = FALSE,   show_outliers = TRUE,   show_inner_points = FALSE )

title: Boxplot chart object — ms_boxplotchart
text: data data.frame. x category column name. unique value becomes one box. y numeric value column name (raw observations). quartile_method one "exclusive" (default) "inclusive". Affects Q1/Q3 computed count even. show_mean_marker logical, draw mean marker. Default TRUE. show_mean_line logical, draw line connecting means across boxes. Default FALSE. show_outliers logical, plot outlier points. Default TRUE. show_inner_points logical, plot non-outlier points. Default FALSE.

title: Combined chart object — ms_chart_combine
text: Combine several chart objects single chart shared axes. chart must named argument. title x-axis label taken first chart. y-axis label first chart secondary axis used secondary y-axis label. one secondary y-axis (right) one secondary x-axis (top) supported.

title: Combined chart object — ms_chart_combine
text: ... named ms_chart objects. secondary_y character vector chart names plot secondary (right) y-axis. secondary_x character vector chart names plot secondary (top) x-axis.

title: Combined chart object — ms_chart_combine
code: # example chart 01 -------  dat <- longley dat$Year <- as.Date(paste0(dat$Year, "-01-01"))  dat_empl <- data.frame(   Year = rep(dat$Year, 2),   value = c(dat$Employed, dat$Unemployed),   serie = rep(c("Employed", "Unemployed"), each = nrow(dat)),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  empl <- ms_barchart(   data = dat_empl, x = "Year",   y = "value", group = "serie" ) empl <- chart_labels(   x = empl, title = "Longley's Economic Regression Data",   xlab = "1947 to 1962", ylab = "Employment" ) empl <- chart_ax_x(x = empl, num_fmt = "yyyy") empl <- as_bar_stack(x = empl, dir = "vertical")  gdp <- ms_linechart(data = dat, x = "Year", y = "GNP.deflator") gdp <- chart_labels(x = gdp, ylab = "GNP implicit price deflator (1954 = 100)")  cb <- ms_chart_combine(empl = empl, gdp = gdp, secondary_y = "gdp")  # example chart 02 ------- # stacked bars + target line on a shared axis (no secondary axis).  browser_data <- data.frame(   browser = rep(c("Android", "Chrome", "IE", "Firefox", "Opera", "Safari"),                 each = 3),   serie   = rep(c("v1", "v2", "v3"), times = 6),   value   = c(80, 90, 77, 150, 140, 131, 60, 70, 62,               50, 60, 52, 40, 50, 42, 55, 65, 55) )  target <- data.frame(   browser = unique(browser_data$browser),   cible   = 220 )  bars <- ms_barchart(browser_data, x = "browser",                     y = "value", group = "serie") bars <- as_bar_stack(bars)  line <- ms_linechart(target, x = "browser", y = "cible") line <- chart_data_stroke(line, values = "#D62728") line <- chart_data_line_width(line, values = 2) line <- chart_data_symbol(line, values = "none")  cb2 <- ms_chart_combine(bars = bars, target = line)  # example chart 03 ------- # two scatter charts on a shared y axis with independent x ranges. # bottom (primary) chart uses temperature in Celsius; # top (secondary_x) chart uses pressure in hPa. # y series must have distinct column names (no collision in the # embedded sheet) — here humidity_t vs humidity_p.  set.seed(1) n <- 20 weather <- data.frame(   temperature_c = runif(n, 5, 30),   pressure_hpa  = runif(n, 990, 1030),   humidity_t    = runif(n, 30, 90),   humidity_p    = runif(n, 30, 90) )  sc_temp <- ms_scatterchart(   data = weather, x = "temperature_c", y = "humidity_t" ) sc_temp <- chart_labels(   sc_temp, title = "Humidity vs temperature and pressure",   xlab = "Temperature (°C)", ylab = "Humidity (%)" )  sc_pres <- ms_scatterchart(   data = weather, x = "pressure_hpa", y = "humidity_p" ) sc_pres <- chart_data_symbol(sc_pres, values = "triangle") sc_pres <- chart_labels(sc_pres, xlab = "Pressure (hPa)")  cb3 <- ms_chart_combine(   temp = sc_temp, pres = sc_pres,   secondary_x = "pres" )

title: Chart Generation for 'Microsoft Word', 'Microsoft Excel' and 'Microsoft PowerPoint' Documents — mschart
text: lets R users create Microsoft Office charts data, add title, legends, annotations chart object. graph produced Microsoft graph, means can edited Microsoft software underlying data available. package allow make charts ggplot2. allows subset charts possible 'Office Chart'. package often used industrialize graphs consumed annotated non-R users. following chart types available. Classical charts (Office 2007+ DrawingML pipeline): bar charts: ms_barchart() line charts: ms_linechart() scatter plots: ms_scatterchart() area charts: ms_areachart() pie doughnut charts: ms_piechart() bubble charts: ms_bubblechart() radar (spider) charts: ms_radarchart() stock charts (HLC OHLC): ms_stockchart() chartEx charts (Office 2016+ pipeline; older viewers show placeholder): box--whisker: ms_boxplotchart() funnel: ms_funnelchart() histogram: ms_histogramchart() pareto: ms_paretochart() sunburst: ms_sunburstchart() treemap: ms_treemapchart() waterfall: ms_waterfallchart() Several classical chart types can combined single chart, optional secondary axis (y x), using ms_chart_combine(). functions create 'chart' object can customized: using options specific chart (chart_settings()), changing options related axes (chart_ax_x() chart_ax_y()), changing options related labels (chart_data_labels()), changing colors, line widths, ... functions chart_labels_text() chart_data_fill() chart_data_line_style() chart_data_line_width() chart_data_size() chart_data_smooth() chart_data_stroke() chart_data_symbol() changing general theme function chart_theme(), changing title labels function chart_labels(). can add chart slide PowerPoint function ph_with.ms_chart(). can add chart Word document function body_add_chart(). can add chart Excel sheet function sheet_add_drawing.ms_chart().

what: Two arguments often confused: asis and write_data
title: Chart Generation for 'Microsoft Word', 'Microsoft Excel' and 'Microsoft PowerPoint' Documents — mschart
text: asis constructor argument (ms_barchart(), ms_linechart() classical constructors). describes input shape data frame: asis = FALSE (default): long format, group column splits rows series. mschart reshapes data internally. asis = TRUE: wide format, one column per series. y accepts vector series column names. write_data embed-time argument sheet_add_drawing.ms_chart() Excel pipeline. decides whether mschart writes chart's data target sheet (TRUE, default) leaves charge placing via officer::sheet_write_data() (FALSE, recommended non-trivial workbooks). two arguments independent: chart built asis = TRUE can still embedded either write_data = TRUE write_data = FALSE.

code: library(officer) mytheme <- mschart_theme(   axis_title = fp_text(color = "red", font.size = 24, bold = TRUE),   grid_major_line_y = fp_border(width = 1, color = "orange"),   axis_ticks_y = fp_border(width = 0.4, color = "gray") )   my_bc <- ms_barchart(   data = browser_data, x = "browser",   y = "value", group = "serie" ) my_bc <- chart_settings(my_bc,   dir = "horizontal", grouping = "stacked",   gap_width = 150, overlap = 100 ) my_bc <- set_theme(my_bc, mytheme)  my_bc_2 <- ms_barchart(   data = browser_data, x = "browser",   y = "value", group = "serie" ) my_bc_2 <- chart_theme(my_bc_2,   grid_major_line_y = fp_border(width = 0.5, color = "cyan") )  # Manual legend layout: place the legend in the top-right corner # using fractions of the chart area (0 to 1). my_bc_3 <- ms_barchart(   data = browser_data, x = "browser",   y = "value", group = "serie" ) my_bc_3 <- chart_theme(my_bc_3,   legend_position = "r",   legend_x = 0.80, legend_y = 0.15,   legend_w = 0.18, legend_h = 0.30 )

title: Add an ms_chart to an Excel sheet — sheet_add_drawing.ms_chart
code: library(officer) library(mschart)  my_chart <- ms_barchart(   data = data.frame(     x = c("A", "B", "C"),     y = c(1, 3, 2),     group = rep("serie1", 3)   ),   x = "x", y = "y", group = "group" )  x <- read_xlsx() x <- add_sheet(x, label = "chart_sheet") x <- sheet_add_drawing(x, value = my_chart, sheet = "chart_sheet") print(x, target = tempfile(fileext = ".xlsx"))  # Sharing one dataset between several charts on the same sheet: # write the data once, then add each chart with write_data = FALSE. shared <- data.frame(   x = c("A", "B", "C"),   y = c(1, 3, 2),   group = rep("serie1", 3) ) chart_a <- ms_barchart(shared, x = "x", y = "y", group = "group") chart_b <- ms_linechart(shared, x = "x", y = "y", group = "group")  x <- read_xlsx() x <- add_sheet(x, label = "multi") x <- sheet_write_data(x, value = chart_a$data_series, sheet = "multi") x <- sheet_add_drawing(x, value = chart_a, sheet = "multi",                        write_data = FALSE,                        left = 3, top = 0.5, width = 5, height = 3.5) x <- sheet_add_drawing(x, value = chart_b, sheet = "multi",                        write_data = FALSE,                        left = 9, top = 0.5, width = 5, height = 3.5) print(x, target = tempfile(fileext = ".xlsx"))

title: mschart 0.5.0
text: Seven new chart types built Office’s chartEx family (Office 2016+): box--whisker (ms_boxplotchart()), funnel (ms_funnelchart()), histogram (ms_histogramchart()), pareto (ms_paretochart()), sunburst (ms_sunburstchart()), treemap (ms_treemapchart()) waterfall (ms_waterfallchart()). Place chart Excel sheet anchored cells. Pass anchor = "B2:H20" sheet_add_drawing() anchor two cells (Excel’s default “move size cells”), anchor = "B2" anchor single cell (move ). Omit anchor absolute placement. edit_as controls Excel rows columns resized.

title: mschart 0.5.0
text: Custom num_fmt strings containing XML metacharacters (e.g. [>=1000] conditional Excel format) longer break chart XML generation; escaped automatically (#98). ms_stockchart() longer fails x column literally named group (x_val / y_val). Internal column names now use .mschart_ prefix avoid collisions user data. ms_chart_combine() supports independent x ranges via secondary_x (top axis), addition existing secondary_y mode. ms_chart_combine() longer produces files PowerPoint refuses open, reports clear errors incompatible inputs (column collisions, x mismatches, unsupported axis combinations).

title: mschart 0.4.3
text: Four new chart types: pie / doughnut (ms_piechart()), bubble (ms_bubblechart()), radar (ms_radarchart()) stock charts HLC OHLC candlesticks (ms_stockchart()). Combine several chart types (e.g. bars + lines) single chart, optional secondary axis, via ms_chart_combine(). Drop chart Excel sheet officer::sheet_add_drawing(); chart data written sheet linked automatically.

title: mschart 0.4.3
